---
title: "Logistic Regression"
author: "Sananthaa J S"
date: "2026-03-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(pROC)      
library(rms) 
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
df <- read.csv("D:/Giloma Grading Mutation Prediction/Glioma-grading-clinical-mutation-prediction/DATA/glioma+grading+clinical+and+mutation+features+dataset/TCGA_InfoWithGrade.csv")

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Grade_f  = factor(Grade, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("LGG","GBM")),
    Gender_f = factor(Gender, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("Male","Female")),
    Race_f   = factor(Race, levels = c(0,1,2,3),
                      labels = c("White","Black/African American",
                                 "Asian","American Indian/Alaska Native"))
  )
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

train_idx <- c(
  sample(which(df$Grade == 0), size = round(0.7 * sum(df$Grade == 0))),
  sample(which(df$Grade == 1), size = round(0.7 * sum(df$Grade == 1)))
)

train <- df[train_idx, ]
test  <- df[-train_idx, ]

cat("Training set:", nrow(train), "\n")
cat("Test set:    ", nrow(test),  "\n")

prop.table(table(train$Grade_f)) * 100
prop.table(table(test$Grade_f))  * 100
```

```{r}
model_lr <- glm(
  Grade ~ Age_at_diagnosis + Gender + Race +
          IDH1 + TP53 + ATRX + PTEN + EGFR + CIC + MUC16 + PIK3CA +
          NF1 + PIK3R1 + FUBP1 + RB1 + NOTCH1 + BCOR + CSMD3 +
          SMARCA4 + GRIN2A + IDH2 + FAT4 + PDGFRA,
  data   = train,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model_lr)
```

#Odds ratios with 95% confidence intervals

```{r }
or_table <- data.frame(
  OR    = exp(coef(model_lr)),
  Lower = exp(confint(model_lr))[, 1],
  Upper = exp(confint(model_lr))[, 2]
) %>%
  round(3) %>%
  filter(rownames(.) != "(Intercept)") %>%
  arrange(OR)

print(or_table)
```

```{r }
# predicted probabilities GBM
pred_prob  <- predict(model_lr, newdata = test, type = "response")

# At 0.5 threshold
pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob >= 0.5, 1, 0)

# confusion matrix
conf_mat <- table(Predicted = pred_class, Actual = test$Grade)
print(conf_mat)

TP <- conf_mat[2, 2]
TN <- conf_mat[1, 1]
FP <- conf_mat[2, 1]
FN <- conf_mat[1, 2]

accuracy    <- (TP + TN) / sum(conf_mat)
sensitivity <- TP / (TP + FN)
specificity <- TN / (TN + FP)

cat("\nAccuracy:", round(accuracy,    3), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity:", round(sensitivity, 3), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(specificity, 3), "\n")
```
#ROC curve and AUC

```{r }
roc_obj <- roc(test$Grade, pred_prob)
auc_val <- auc(roc_obj)
cat("AUC:", round(auc_val, 3), "\n")

plot(roc_obj,
     col  = "#3B8BD4",
     lwd  = 2,
     main = paste0("ROC curve — logistic regression (AUC = ",
                   round(auc_val, 3), ")"))
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey60")
```

---

#Brier score

```{r }
brier <- mean((pred_prob - test$Grade)^2)
cat("Brier score:", round(brier, 4), "\n")

null_brier <- mean((mean(train$Grade) - test$Grade)^2)
cat("Null Brier:", round(null_brier, 4), "\n")
cat("Brier skill:", round(1 - brier / null_brier, 3),
    "(higher = better than null)\n")
```

---

#Calibration curve
From syllabus §7: calibration asks whether predicted probabilities match
observed event rates. A perfectly calibrated model sits on the diagonal.
The calibration slope should be ~1 and intercept ~0.

```{r }
val.prob(pred_prob, test$Grade)
```

```{r }
cat("Results\n")
cat("Logistic regression, all 23 predictors\n")
cat("Train n:", nrow(train), "\n")
cat("Test n:", nrow(test),  "\n")
cat("AUC:", round(auc_val, 3), "\n")
cat("Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 3), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity:", round(sensitivity, 3), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(specificity, 3), "\n")
cat("Brier score:", round(brier, 4), "\n")
cat("Null Brier:", round(null_brier, 4), "\n")
```

NOTES:

Stratified 70/30 split preserving class balance
Fitted logistic regression with all 23 predictors (no selection yet)
Evaluated with confusion matrix, sensitivity, specificity, AUC, Brier score, and calibration curve 
Added null Brier score as a reference — any useful model must beat this

Logistic regression is the natural baseline for binary outcomes
Odds ratios make it interpretable (IDH1 OR will be very large)
AUC measures discrimination; Brier score measures probability accuracy
Calibration tells us whether probabilities are trustworthy (this mattersclinically)


Apply lasso and ridge regularisation
